projects.title: trale
projects.description: A privacy-first body-weight diary for Android. It tracks your weight over time with smart trend smoothing and gap interpolation, wrapped in a clean Material design. Your data never leaves the device — no accounts, no tracking, no cloud.

projects.title: prettypyplot
projects.description: A lightweight wrapper around matplotlib that makes publication-quality figures the default. Sensible color cycles, consistent styling, and one-line helpers take the fiddling out of plotting so you can focus on the data rather than the axes.

projects.title: fontawesome
projects.description: A LaTeX package giving you access to the full Font Awesome 7 (and 6) icon sets directly in your documents, presentations, and posters. Published on CTAN and compatible with PDFLaTeX, XeLaTeX, and LuaLaTeX, in both regular and solid styles.

research.title: Fokker-Planck Score Learning
research.description: A machine-learning method for efficient free-energy estimation under periodic boundary conditions. It trains a diffusion-style score model on the Fokker-Planck dynamics, delivering accurate estimates in regimes where conventional approaches struggle.

research.title: msmhelper
research.description: A time-series analysis toolkit for building and validating Markov state models of molecular dynamics. It offers fast, Numba-accelerated routines for clustering, dynamical coring, and pathway analysis of long trajectories.

research.title: dcTMD
research.description: An analysis framework for dissipation-corrected targeted molecular dynamics. It reconstructs free-energy profiles and friction factors along a pulled reaction coordinate from nonequilibrium pulling simulations.

research.title: MoSAIC
research.description: A correlation-based feature selection method that groups collective coordinates by their mutual dependencies. It uncovers the essential degrees of freedom in high-dimensional data fully unsupervised, without prior assumptions.

research.title: NorMI
research.description: A generalized Kraskov estimator for the normalized mutual information of multidimensional data. It quantifies nonlinear dependencies robustly, staying accurate even in high dimensions and with limited samples.
